title: Citrus solid perfume
subtitle: A pocket-sized solid perfume with sweet orange and lemon essential oils
excerpt: A small anhydrous solid perfume made from beeswax and jojoba oil, scented with sweet orange and lemon essential oils. Carry in a small tin; apply with a fingertip to the wrists or neck. The fragrance is subtle compared to an alcohol-based perfume, which suits those who prefer a lighter scent.
sourceNotes: Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014): sweet orange and lemon essential oils at solid perfume concentrations for leave-on skin application. Anhydrous solid perfume base ratios: standard formulation.

recipe.yieldDescription: 2 x 15 ml salve tins (~30 g total)
recipe.batchNotes: Double the batch for four tins. Give the extra tins as gifts.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make ahead; solid perfume is stable and benefits from a few days for the fragrance to settle into the wax.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months from making. Citrus essential oils, especially lemon, are top notes that fade faster than base notes. The sweet orange will outlast the lemon in the tin. The beeswax and jojoba are stable indefinitely. Discard if the perfume develops a rancid smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: A solid perfume uses a wax-and-oil base instead of the alcohol used in conventional liquid perfume. The essential oil concentration is typically 15 to 30% of the total base weight. Because a solid perfume is applied by fingertip rather than atomised, the amount deposited on the skin is small; 20% EO in a wax base gives a similar effective skin dose to a 10 to 15% eau de parfum sprayed once.

Citrus essential oils such as lemon and sweet orange are top notes: they are the first fragrance compounds to evaporate, both from the tin and from the skin after application. A citrus solid perfume will smell strongly of lemon immediately after opening but the base note of sweet orange will dominate within 30 minutes on the skin. The tin's overall fragrance will shift toward sweet orange as the months pass.

content.text: A solid perfume fits in a pocket or handbag and applies cleanly without the alcohol sting of a spray. The

of 20% essential oil by base weight gives a real fragrance impact, though the overall sillage (the scent trail in the air) is quieter than a sprayed perfume. Apply with a fingertip to pulse points; the warmth of the skin helps the wax to melt and release the fragrance.

both in the tin and on skin. A freshly made tin will smell more lemon-forward than one made 3 months ago. Avoid applying to the face or broken skin; the essential oil load is high for a leave-on product and citrus oils can cause sensitivity in some individuals. Do a patch test on the inner forearm before use.

items.prepNote: Cosmetic-grade beeswax. 26% of base weight gives a firm solid that softens from skin heat but holds its shape in the tin.
Provides the carrier oil phase. Very stable and slow to oxidise, which is important as the tin will be opened repeatedly.
16% of total base weight. Cold-pressed sweet orange peel oil. The primary warm, round note.
8% of total base weight. Adds brightness and sharpness above the sweet orange. Add at below 40 degrees Celsius to preserve the volatile top notes.

content.text: Weigh the beeswax pellets and jojoba oil into the heatproof metal bowl. Set over a pan of simmering water and stir until the beeswax is fully melted. Remove from the heat and allow to cool to below 40 degrees Celsius.

Stir in the sweet orange and lemon essential oils. Pour quickly into the salve tins before the wax begins to set. The small tins will set rapidly; work fast once the essential oils are added. Leave undisturbed for 30 minutes until fully set.

Keep in the sealed tin at room temperature, away from heat sources that would melt the wax. Carries well in a handbag or pocket as long as ambient temperatures stay below 30 degrees Celsius. Shelf life is 12 months; the lemon top note fades faster than the sweet orange. Discard if the wax develops a rancid smell.
